title: Modified HMW DNA Isolation from Stramenopiles with Agar Plugs
author: Vincent A Bielinski
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.wenfbde
materials: Let cells sit on ice 1 min
Allow tube to cool to RT
Invert tube slowly 10 times until mixed

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 g of LMP agarose to 25 mL TAE (2% final)

steps.title: Microwave for 30 secs or until solution begins to bubble
steps.instruction: Microwave for 30 secs or until solution begins to bubble

steps.title: Repeat heating steps as necessary (2-3x)
steps.instruction: Repeat heating steps as necessary (2-3x)

steps.title: Move flask to 50 o C...
steps.instruction: Move flask to 50 o C air incubator, swirling a few times to bring agarose into solution

steps.title: Thaw 50 mL conical vial with...
steps.instruction: Thaw 50 mL conical vial with cell pellets on ice (cell pellet ~1.5g for this expt)

steps.title: Add 50 mL 1M sorbitol and resuspend pellet gently
steps.instruction: Add 50 mL 1M sorbitol and resuspend pellet gently

steps.title: Harvest pellet with 10 min spin...
steps.instruction: Harvest pellet with 10 min spin at 3000 x g at 4 o C

steps.title: Remove sorbitol wash supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove sorbitol wash supernatant

steps.title: Resuspend cells in 5 mL protoplasting solution
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells in 5 mL protoplasting solution

steps.title: Incubate cell suspension at 37 o...
steps.instruction: Incubate cell suspension at 37 o C for 10 min, then remove from water bath

steps.title: Keeping agarose warm, mix 5 mL...
steps.instruction: Keeping agarose warm, mix 5 mL of 2% agarose with cell suspension

steps.title: Pipette 100 uL of cell-agar solution...
steps.instruction: Pipette 100 uL of cell-agar solution into disposable plug molds (Biorad #170-3713) Note: Wide-bore tips can make it easier to dispense agar-cell solution into the molds if regular tips keep clogging

steps.title: Place molds at 4 o C...
steps.instruction: Place molds at 4 o C for 15-30 min to solidify

steps.title: Once solid, transfer plugs to new...
steps.instruction: Once solid, transfer plugs to new 50 mL conical vial Note: The plug molds come with a comb for pushing the plugs out into the new conical vial

steps.title: Wash plugs with 25 mL of...
steps.instruction: Wash plugs with 25 mL of wash buffer (30 min at RT with soft shaking), the tube can be taped on its side on an orbital rocker and mixed slowly

steps.title: Add 10-20 mL of proteinase K solution Note
steps.instruction: Add 10-20 mL of proteinase K solution Note: This enzyme can be expensive, so scale volumes accordingly. You really just need enough to cover the plugs.

steps.title: Incubate for 24 hrs at 50 o C Note
steps.instruction: Incubate for 24 hrs at 50 o C Note: At this point you should see cell lysis and your solution change colors. For diatoms, the solution becomes green within 30 minutes, then changes to brown over the 24 hrs.

steps.title: Wash plugs 2x with 25 mL...
steps.instruction: Wash plugs 2x with 25 mL of wash buffer (30 min at RT)

steps.title: Add 10-20 mL of Pronase solution to plugs Note
steps.instruction: Add 10-20 mL of Pronase solution to plugs Note: This step can be omitted if you are confident that the Proteinase K treatment is sufficient for your organism, but will not harm your DNA if left in.

steps.title: Incubate plugs in Pronase solution for...
steps.instruction: Incubate plugs in Pronase solution for 4 hrs at 37 o C

steps.title: Wash plugs 2x with 25 mL...
steps.instruction: Wash plugs 2x with 25 mL wash buffer (30 min at RT)

steps.title: Store at 4 o C or...
steps.instruction: Store at 4 o C or proceed to next step Note: Plugs can be left in wash buffer after this step for up to 1 month at 4 o C

steps.title: For agarose digestion, we often process...
steps.instruction: For agarose digestion, we often process a small amount of the total plugs to first determine DNA yield and often this small number of plugs (~5) can yield enough DNA for a Nanopore run

steps.title: To digest, remove 3-4 plugs (or...
steps.instruction: To digest, remove 3-4 plugs (or more) from conical vial and transfer into new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube

steps.title: Wash 2x with 500 uL of...
steps.instruction: Wash 2x with 500 uL of 1x beta-agarase wash buffer (NEB) on ice for 30 min each cycle Note: the NEB buffer is composed of 10 mM Bis-Tris-HCl, 1 mM EDTA, pH 6.5@25°C if you want to make it in-house

steps.title: Remove excess wash buffer from tube with pipette
steps.instruction: Remove excess wash buffer from tube with pipette

steps.title: Heat tube at 65 o C...
steps.instruction: Heat tube at 65 o C for 10 min in heating block

steps.title: Cool block and solution to 42 o C Note
steps.instruction: Cool block and solution to 42 o C Note: Leave to cool at least 10 min, as agarase gets inactivated over 45 o C

steps.title: Add 1U Beta-agarase per 100 uL melted agarose volume
steps.instruction: Add 1U Beta-agarase per 100 uL melted agarose volume

steps.title: Incubate for 1-2 hr at 42 o C Note
steps.instruction: Incubate for 1-2 hr at 42 o C Note: Tubes can be gently shaken during treatment, but leaving on bench and mixing every 30 min should work as well

steps.title: Store at 4 o C overnight...
steps.instruction: Store at 4 o C overnight or move on to extraction (below)

steps.title: Phenol extraction for HMW DNA Note
steps.instruction: Phenol extraction for HMW DNA Note: Wear proper PPE and work with phenol in a fume hood as much as possible

steps.title: Mix 250 uL of liquefied agar...
steps.instruction: Mix 250 uL of liquefied agar solution with 250 uL of Tris-buffered phenol

steps.title: Rock end over end slowly for...
steps.instruction: Rock end over end slowly for 10 min at RT Note: Extractions can go for up to an hour at RT if greater DNA yield is desired

steps.title: Separate phases by centrifuging for 10...
steps.instruction: Separate phases by centrifuging for 10 min at 16,100 x g

steps.title: Transfer aqueous layer (around 250 ul) to new tube
steps.instruction: Transfer aqueous layer (around 250 ul) to new tube

steps.title: Add 25 ul of 3M NaOAC to tube Note
steps.instruction: Add 25 ul of 3M NaOAC to tube Note: We’ve noticed that addition of the 3M NaOAc causes the aqueous layer to become turbid and opaque, so if you see this it’s not unusual

steps.title: Add 825 uL of 100% EtOH
steps.instruction: Add 825 uL of 100% EtOH

steps.title: Allow to sit at -20 o...
steps.instruction: Allow to sit at -20 o C for 1 hr minimum, overnight precipitations may increase DNA yield

steps.title: Centrifuge for 20 min at 16,100...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 20 min at 16,100 x g at RT Note: make sure to have the centrifuge set to lowest acceleration/deceleration speed possible if you want to avoid shearing

steps.title: Remove supernatant gently with pipet
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant gently with pipet

steps.title: Wash pellet quickly in 500 uL...
steps.instruction: Wash pellet quickly in 500 uL 70% EtOH + 50mM CaCl 2 Note: We have found adding calcium can help precipitate out carbohydrates or lipids often associated with the gDNA

steps.title: Centrifuge for 5 min at 16,100 x g at RT
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 5 min at 16,100 x g at RT

steps.title: Repeat 70% EtOH + 50 mM CaCl 2 wash
steps.instruction: Repeat 70% EtOH + 50 mM CaCl 2 wash

steps.title: Centrifuge for 5 min at 16,100 x g at RT
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 5 min at 16,100 x g at RT

steps.title: Remove wash solution with pipette
steps.instruction: Remove wash solution with pipette

steps.title: Allow pellet to air dry for 5-10 min at RT
steps.instruction: Allow pellet to air dry for 5-10 min at RT

steps.title: Resuspend pellet gently in 50-100 uL TE, pH 8
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet gently in 50-100 uL TE, pH 8.0 - DO NOT MIX WITH PIPETTE Note: If you have trouble resuspending your pellet, you can either leave it at 4 o C overnight or heat the tube to 37 o C for about 10 minutes to help solubilize DNA

steps.title: Analyze 5 ul of resuspended pellet on 0
steps.instruction: Analyze 5 ul of resuspended pellet on 0.8% agarose gel to determine DNA quality, using wide-bore tips to transfer DNA solution to gel while preventing shearing Note: We currently use Qubit quantitation for measuring the concentration of HMW DNA
